title: Limniris chrysophylla
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Limniris chrysophylla. However, I found that the plant you are referring to might be Limonium chrysophyllum, which is an annual or biennial plant species in the Plumbaginaceae family, commonly known as sea lavender. 

Here's a short paragraph about the plant Limonium chrysophyllum:

Limonium chrysophyllum, also known as sea lavender, is a coastal plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support. The bioactive constituents of Limonium chrysophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limonium chrysophyllum are teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions and skin issues.
